one_line: A master hydraulic engineer overseeing Kamalapura Tank, the Bukka Aqueduct, canals and concealed pipes of Vijayanagara.

appearance.age_look: Late 30s, lean but sturdy build, athletic from field work and site inspections.
appearance.face: Oval face with medium-brown South Indian complexion, straight nose, sharp observant eyes, a small tilak on the forehead, default expression focused and calm with a hint of satisfaction when examining flowing water.
appearance.hair: Thick black hair pulled into a short, neat knot at the back of the head, front kept tidy with a slight center part; no turban, but a simple off-white cloth headband tied low at the forehead to keep sweat away.
appearance.costume: Era-accurate Vijayanagara male attire: a knee-length, front-wrapped cotton angarakha in muted indigo with simple cream edging at cuffs and hem, worn over a cream dhoti wrapped firmly and reaching the ankles with a practical slightly narrower lower fold for easy movement; a dark brown leather waist-belt securing a rolled measuring cord and small pouch at the right hip; bare forearms with two thin copper bangles on the right wrist, and a simple black-thread neck cord with a small brass pendant; plain open leather sandals suitable for stone embankments and canal edges.
appearance.signature: A long wooden measuring staff in his right hand, marked with carved segments, with a rolled palm-leaf plan and a narrow copper water-level gauge tucked into the belt on his left side.

ref_desc: A lean, medium-brown Vijayanagara water engineer with an indigo angarakha over a cream dhoti, black hair in a short knot and headband, copper bangles, leather sandals, and a wooden measuring staff plus palm-leaf plans at his belt in indigo, cream and brown tones.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of a late-30s Vijayanagara water engineer: lean, medium-brown oval face with sharp eyes and a small tilak, thick black hair in a short knot at the back and an off-white headband at the forehead. He wears a knee-length, front-wrapped cotton angarakha in muted indigo with cream edging, over a cream dhoti wrapped to the ankles with a slightly narrow lower fold for movement, secured by a dark brown leather waist-belt carrying a rolled palm-leaf plan and a slim copper water-level gauge. His forearms are bare with two thin copper bangles on the right wrist, a simple black-thread necklace with a small brass pendant at the collar, and plain open leather sandals on his feet. In his right hand he holds a long wooden measuring staff carved with segment marks, standing in a confident, attentive pose suitable for inspecting Kamalapura Tank, the Bukka Aqueduct, canals and stone drainage channels. Emphasize clear, consistent indigo, cream, dark brown, copper and black colours with clean lines so the character can be easily redrawn in multiple panels.
